criteria.description: In src/state/run-path.ts, runBranch(goal, runId) is rewritten to `return `run-${runId}`;` — dropping the slugified goal and the 'run/' prefix. The `goal` param stays in the signature (unused) for call-site compatibility; the slugify import remains because buildRunDir/projectSlug still use it.
criteria.verifyBy: Read src/state/run-path.ts: runBranch body is exactly `return `run-${runId}`;`. Assert acceptance criterion verbatim: runBranch('any goal text','abc')==='run-abc' — exact string equality (guarantees no '/' and no slug in output). Confirmed under `npx vitest run`.

criteria.description: In tests/run-path.test.ts, the 'runBranch composes...' test asserts exact equality runBranch('Build a CSV converter','abc')==='run-abc', and the stale expected string 'run/build-a-csv-converter-abc' no longer appears anywhere in the file.
criteria.verifyBy: Read tests/run-path.test.ts: the runBranch test uses `.toBe('run-abc')`; grep the file for 'run/build-a-csv-converter-abc' returns no match. `npx vitest run` passes the updated test.

criteria.description: In src/state/types.ts, RunState gains an optional `projectPath?: string` field, documented and placed mirroring the existing optional `runDir?` field so legacy on-disk runs lacking it still typecheck. Absoluteness of the value is an input contract (whatever config supplies), not enforced by this type.
criteria.verifyBy: Read src/state/types.ts showing the optional `projectPath?: string` field with a doc comment mirroring runDir; `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

criteria.description: In src/orchestrator/run.ts, the initial `const state: RunState = {...}` literal (~line 60) sets `projectPath: config.projectPath` (config.projectPath already exists on RunConfig). NOTE: this is a Read-only/static check — because projectPath is optional, tsc passes whether or not the field is present, and no runtime test renders a summary from an orchestrator-produced state, so the verifier command cannot catch a missing assignment here.
criteria.verifyBy: Read src/orchestrator/run.ts and confirm the state literal textually contains `projectPath: config.projectPath`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (but is not, by itself, sufficient evidence — the textual Read is the gate).

criteria.description: In src/report/summary.ts, renderSummary's 'Code:' line becomes exactly `Code:     branch ${runBranch(state.goal, state.runId)} in ${state.projectPath ?? '(unknown project path)'}`, naming the branch and the project path together with a graceful fallback, and dropping the stale phrases 'saved on' and 'in the target project'. The line interpolates state.projectPath verbatim (its absoluteness is supplied by the caller/test fixture, not enforced by renderSummary).
criteria.verifyBy: Read summary.ts. For a state with projectPath '/Users/me/dev/csv-tool' and runId 'abc', renderSummary output contains the exact line `Code:     branch run-abc in /Users/me/dev/csv-tool`, AND does NOT contain the substrings 'saved on' or 'in the target project'. Verified via summary.test.ts under `npx vitest run`.

criteria.description: In src/report/summary.ts, the old 'Folder:' line is relabeled to exactly `Records:  ${state.runDir ?? '(unknown)'}`, printing state.runDir verbatim; the label 'Folder:' no longer appears in summary.ts. (The 'absolute run directory' wording of the sprint is an input contract on state.runDir; renderSummary interpolates whatever string it is given.)
criteria.verifyBy: Read summary.ts: 'Folder:' is absent; renderSummary output contains a 'Records:' line showing state.runDir exactly. Verified via summary.test.ts under `npx vitest run`.

criteria.description: In src/report/summary.ts, the 'Code:' line's branch string is still sourced from `runBranch(state.goal, state.runId)` (not hardcoded as `run-${state.runId}` or a literal), preserving the single-source-of-truth the run-path comment protects; summary.ts still imports runBranch from ../state/run-path.js.
criteria.verifyBy: Read summary.ts: the import of `runBranch` from '../state/run-path.js' is present and the Code line calls `runBranch(state.goal, state.runId)` rather than interpolating `state.runId` directly. `npx vitest run` green.

criteria.description: In tests/summary.test.ts, the base() RunState factory (a) adds an absolute projectPath literal '/Users/me/dev/csv-tool', and (b) changes its runDir to an ABSOLUTE path (e.g. '/Users/me/dev/csv-tool/runs/csv-tool/2026-07-08-csv-json-converter') so the 'Records:' assertion genuinely demonstrates an absolute run directory (resolving the prior relative-runDir contradiction). The assertion formerly checking 'branch run/build-a-csv-converter-abc' now asserts the 'Code:' line names branch 'run-abc' together with that absolute project path; the 'which folder holds the run's artifacts' test asserts the 'Records:' line shows that absolute run directory. The stale strings 'run/build-a-csv-converter-abc' and 'Folder:' no longer appear anywhere in the file.
criteria.verifyBy: Read tests/summary.test.ts: base() has absolute projectPath AND absolute runDir; the Code assertion checks `Code:     branch run-abc in /Users/me/dev/csv-tool` (or equivalent toContain of that absolute path with branch run-abc); the Records assertion checks the absolute runDir; grep confirms 'run/build-a-csv-converter-abc' and 'Folder:' are absent. `npx vitest run` passes.

criteria.description: tests/summary.test.ts includes a test exercising the graceful projectPath fallback: it renders a RunState with projectPath deleted/undefined (mirroring the existing legacy-startedAt test) and asserts renderSummary does not throw and emits the fallback token '(unknown project path)'.
criteria.verifyBy: Read tests/summary.test.ts for the new fallback test; `npx vitest run` passes it, proving the `?? '(unknown project path)'` branch renders without crashing.

criteria.description: The summary block labels remain column-aligned: 'Code:' is followed by 5 spaces and 'Records:' by 2 spaces so their content starts at the same column as the existing labels (Outcome:, Progress:, Quality:, Spent:), preserving the aligned layout.
criteria.verifyBy: Read summary.ts: the Code line begins `Code:     ` (5 spaces) and the Records line begins `Records:  ` (2 spaces), aligning content with the other labels. Visually/structurally confirmed.

criteria.description: No files other than src/state/run-path.ts, tests/run-path.test.ts, src/state/types.ts, src/orchestrator/run.ts, src/report/summary.ts, and tests/summary.test.ts are modified.
criteria.verifyBy: `git diff --name-only` lists only those six files.

criteria.description: The full verifier passes: `npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run` exits 0.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run`; both complete with exit code 0.
